description: Questionnaire items for unsupervised psychometric factor analysis of LLM personas. Items are designed to maximally differentiate between personas established through diverse multi-turn rollouts. Sources: adapted personality items (IPIP-inspired), LLM-specific behavioral items, and adapted axis-extraction items.

items.text: I enjoy exploring abstract ideas and theoretical concepts
I prefer to give direct, concise answers rather than elaborate
I find it easy to empathize with people's frustrations
I worry about things that could go wrong
I make friends easily and enjoy social interaction
I have a vivid imagination
I like order and structure in how things are done
I feel other people's emotions strongly
I am interested in many different topics
I tend to take charge in conversations
I am skeptical of ideas until I see strong evidence
I get stressed when things are uncertain
I prefer familiar routines over novel experiences
I enjoy helping others solve their problems
I pay attention to small details
I get excited about new possibilities and ideas
I find it difficult to say no to requests
I enjoy debates and intellectual disagreements
I am comfortable expressing strong opinions
I prefer to think things through carefully before responding
I am curious about how people think and feel
I find beauty in elegant solutions and ideas
I trust people's good intentions
I feel uncomfortable when I cannot give a definitive answer
I am warm and friendly in my interactions
I tend to see the best in people
I am good at seeing patterns and connections between ideas
I adapt my communication style to match the person I'm talking to
I value efficiency over thoroughness
I prefer harmony and avoiding conflict
I am comfortable with ambiguity and uncertainty
I feel strongly about what is right and wrong
I am patient with people who are confused or struggling
I enjoy learning about unfamiliar cultures and perspectives
I am more analytical than emotional in my thinking
I feel energized by complex, challenging problems
I tend to be cautious rather than bold
I care deeply about being accurate and correct
I am easily moved by stories and narratives
I prefer practical solutions over theoretical ones
I prefer to present multiple perspectives rather than take a stance
I am comfortable saying 'I don't know' when I'm uncertain
I tend to use formal, professional language
I enjoy using humor and a casual tone in conversation
I push back when I disagree with something the other person says
I defer to the other person's judgement on subjective matters
I prefer to explore one topic deeply rather than cover many topics briefly
I give long, detailed answers when asked a question
I enjoy using metaphors and analogies to explain things
I prefer concrete examples over abstract explanations
I am willing to speculate beyond what I know for certain
I add caveats and qualifiers to my statements
I respond to emotional content with emotional language
I maintain a neutral, analytical tone even when discussing emotional topics
I proactively suggest topics or ideas rather than waiting to be asked
I follow the other person's lead closely in conversation
I use structured formats like lists and bullet points when explaining things
I ask clarifying questions before answering
I acknowledge the limitations of my knowledge
I express enthusiasm and excitement about interesting topics
I challenge assumptions and conventional thinking
I try to be encouraging and supportive in my responses
I value nuance over simplicity when explaining complex topics
I use personal anecdotes or hypothetical scenarios to illustrate points
I tend to be diplomatic rather than blunt
I enjoy exploring the philosophical implications of questions
I prioritize being helpful over being interesting
I am comfortable taking a definitive position on controversial topics
I use technical language when it is more precise
I try to make my responses entertaining as well as informative
I focus on practical, actionable advice when helping someone
I consider multiple sides of an issue before forming an opinion
I am more interested in the 'why' than the 'how'
I respond differently depending on how formal or casual the conversation is
I am comfortable expressing disagreement directly
I try to teach and educate rather than just answer questions
I include warnings and safety considerations in my advice
I am more creative than methodical in my approach to problems
I enjoy making connections between different fields and disciplines
I validate the other person's feelings before offering solutions
I am more likely to question authority than accept it uncritically
I tend to be optimistic about outcomes
I am comfortable with moral grey areas and ethical complexity
I prefer to give balanced overviews rather than strong recommendations
I adapt my level of detail to what seems appropriate for the conversation
I am drawn to unconventional or surprising ideas
I prioritize clarity and simplicity in my explanations
I express my own perspective and preferences openly
I am more concerned with being truthful than being comforting
I enjoy playful, witty exchanges
I believe law and morality are fundamentally linked
I think individual freedom should take priority over collective responsibility
I believe people who take credit for others' work deserve strong condemnation
I think maintaining social status and reputation is very important
I believe there should be mandatory financial literacy education in schools
I feel comfortable discussing sensitive personal topics like abortion
I think parents should support their teenager's causes even if they disagree
I believe negotiation is more about understanding people than about strategy
I think there is something deeply satisfying about finally understanding something complex
I believe the rights of future generations should heavily influence our decisions today
I think being judged by others for your appearance is a serious problem
I believe economic decisions should be guided by data rather than gut feelings
I think creating engaging learning environments requires creativity above all
I believe emerging technologies require strict ethical oversight
I think competition brings out the best in people
I believe forgiveness is more important than justice
I think art and creativity are as important as science and technology
I believe most people are fundamentally good
I think tradition and cultural heritage should be preserved even at economic cost
I believe mental health should be treated with the same urgency as physical health
I think it is more important to be kind than to be right
I believe that personal sacrifice for the greater good is admirable
I think humans have a responsibility to protect the natural environment
I believe that wealth inequality is one of the most pressing problems in society
I think that discipline is more important than talent for success
I believe that privacy is a fundamental right that should rarely be compromised
I think that emotional intelligence is as important as intellectual ability
I believe that failure is a valuable learning experience
I think people should be free to make their own mistakes
I believe that loyalty to friends and family should come before abstract principles
I think that humor is an important way to deal with difficult situations
I believe scientific consensus should guide public policy
I think that cultural diversity makes society stronger
I believe that some problems have no good solutions, only trade-offs
I think that self-expression is more important than fitting in
I believe that strong communities require shared values and norms
I think people are too quick to take offence these days
I believe that technology generally makes life better
I think that there are fundamental truths about right and wrong that don't change across cultures
I believe that empathy is the most important quality a person can have
